title: • If you want a tragic, aging mentor figure → The Wrestler
content:
• If you want a tragic, aging mentor figure → The Wrestler
• If you want music-focused rivalry → Amadeus
• If you want a survivalist metaphor for discipline → The Pianist
• If you want classic perseverance & underdog grit → Rocky

Would you prefer a brutally realistic, psychological, or heroic struggle as your fourth influence?

Character Types in This Genre (Archetypes & Their Roles)

Since your inspirations (Whiplash, Real, Vagabond) are character-driven, we need to break down the essential archetypes found in stories about obsession, competition, and self-mastery.

1. The Protagonist (The Obsessed / The Master in Training)

This character is defined by obsession—they are willing to sacrifice their relationships, health, and sanity for a singular goal.

Examples:
• Andrew (Whiplash) – A drummer who destroys himself to reach an unreachable standard.
• Musashi (Vagabond) – A samurai who believes raw strength is everything, only to learn mastery is internal.
context: From document: Analyzing the Common Threads in Whiplash, Real, and Vagabond — chunk 10 (chunk 10/14)
